name: Speed up tmux-sessionx popup render latency

description: Establish baseline popup benchmark and functional checks using a real tmux popup-launch benchmark plus mocked scenario regression checks.

Avoid repeated session list work before launching the popup by skipping custom-path session scans when no custom paths are configured, reusing the same sorted session list across input builders, and removing the duplicate additional_input call in handle_input.

Bundle popup-launch runtime settings into one cached tmux option built by sessionx.tmux, then hydrate those values with a single tmux call in scripts/sessionx.sh instead of many per-popup tmux show-option lookups.
Collapse popup-launch state hydration to a single tmux show-option call by caching args, fzf options, and runtime settings together in one built state payload.
Tried simplifying get_sorted_sessions with tmux's session-name format and direct printing instead of sed/awk post-processing, but popup launch got slower on the benchmark.
Tried lazy-loading tmuxinator, fzf-marks, and git-branch helpers only in handle_output, but popup launch regressed slightly on the real benchmark.

Verification benchmark on the current best code while running inside tmux. Result is slightly faster than the previous best but there were no code changes, so treat it as measurement variance rather than a new improvement.
Tried replacing get_sorted_sessions and current-session filtering with pure Bash loops to remove sed/grep/awk from the hot path, but the real popup-launch benchmark regressed.
Tried replacing the pre-fzf echo/sed/tail pipeline with printf, Bash string substitution, and in-shell last-line extraction to cut process startup, but the popup-launch benchmark regressed.

Combine current-session and last-session lookup into one tmux display-message call per popup launch, cache those values in the shell runtime, and update the profiler harness to mirror the combined format.
Tried switching the session list call to tmux's session-name format to avoid the sed strip step, but popup launch got slower than the current best.
Tried removing dead helper functions from scripts/sessionx.sh to shrink parse overhead on launch, but the real popup benchmark regressed versus the current best.

Replace the generic fzf-tmux popup wrapper on the non-builtin popup path with a lean SessionX-specific tmux popup helper that preserves behavior and falls back to fzf-tmux if direct popup execution cannot safely complete.
Tried deferring tmuxinator, fzf-marks, and git-branch helper sourcing until after popup selection, but the isolated popup-launch benchmark regressed versus the new direct-popup helper baseline.

Tried inlining the direct-popup helper command to avoid writing a temporary popup script file, but the follow-up benchmark with more samples showed the small apparent gain was just noise and the median regressed.
Tried folding current-session filtering into get_sorted_sessions to remove the extra hot-path grep, but the isolated popup benchmark regressed relative to the direct-popup helper baseline.

Tried a carefully gated builtin fzf --tmux fast path with fallback to the current direct-popup helper and fzf-tmux, and updated the isolated benchmark harness to simulate builtin popup launches. Larger-sample validation showed the path regressed relative to the current best.
Tried replacing the per-launch temporary popup script with a static repo runner script plus environment variables, but the direct-popup benchmark regressed relative to the current best.
Tried removing the now-redundant awk de-dup step in get_sorted_sessions after last-session exclusion, but the direct-popup benchmark regressed versus the current best.
Tried moving popup sizing out of the cached fzf args so the direct-popup helper no longer had to strip size flags on launch, but the benchmark regressed relative to the current best.

Tried a streaming popup helper that spools stdin to a file in the background and feeds the popup via tail -f so popup launch can overlap input capture while preserving fzf-tmux fallback. Larger-sample validation showed it was still slightly slower than the current best.

Tried removing the helper's extra status file by relying on tmux popup -E to propagate the popup command exit code and using output-file existence to detect successful popup execution, but larger-sample validation still regressed slightly versus the current best.

Tried caching a helper-specific fzf argument array with popup sizing already stripped so the direct-popup helper could skip argument reparsing on each launch, but the benchmark regressed versus the current best.
